round_note: Round 2 /close attempt. Round 1 changed-files were .py-only (out-of-domain). Round 2 dispatch adds commands/commit.md to the changed-files list — IN domain. This report supersedes the Round 1 not_applicable verdict with a Round-2 in-domain analysis.
scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings only for the listed files and tags each with introduced_in_diff:true|false per AC-2.6 default-safe rule. The inspector's domain is ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md; non-Markdown changed-files are tagged out-of-domain with no findings synthesized.

inspector_scope.principle: rules not stories — applied to Markdown command/agent documentation only

load_bearing_rules_conveyed: Resolution order: subproject docs/dev/ first, then /root/docs/dev/ fallback
Exit-code contract: helper exits 1 when no candidate file exists
Variable state on error: CLOSE_REPORT still holds fallback path so check 1 can emit standard error message

removed_block.location: Step 3, formerly 2 lines of inline bash
removed_block.content: CONTROL_ROOT=/root + CLOSE_REPORT assignment to /root path only (no subproject probe)

verbose_section_scan.note: No critical-pattern section headers exist anywhere in the file. Structure is procedural (Step 1-7 + short reference notes).

in_domain_analysis.verdict_rationale: The diff is a substantive procedural simplification: ~16 lines of inline bash path-walk logic were extracted to scripts/resolve-close-report.sh and replaced with a 1-line helper invocation plus a 5-line prose paragraph documenting the helper's contract (resolution order, exit code, error-state variable retention). All 5 lines of the added prose convey load-bearing rules — no philosophical drift, no storytelling, no anti-pattern documentation creep, no Philosophy/Overview/Quality Standards/Safety Features section creation. The 'rules not stories' principle is satisfied: each sentence is a contract clause an implementer needs in order to use the helper correctly. The diff is a net cleanliness improvement (removes inline bash; adds short rule-only prose).

out_of_domain_findings.note: prompt-inspector domain is ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only; .py files are out of scope. Code/style review belongs to a separate inspector role.
prompt-inspector domain is ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only; .py files are out of scope.
prompt-inspector domain is ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only; .py test files are out of scope.
prompt-inspector domain is ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md only; .sh files are out of scope.

summary.note: Single in-domain file (commands/commit.md) inspected. Diff is a substantive simplification with no verbosity violations introduced. Findings list intentionally empty; close.md sees nothing from prompt-inspector to block on.

new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.name: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.description: In --changed-files mode, prompt-inspector emits findings for the listed files and tags each with an explicit introduced_in_diff:bool field. close.md uses this tag per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule.
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.applied_in_this_report_detail: The single in-domain file (commands/commit.md) yielded zero verbosity findings, so no finding-level introduced_in_diff tags were emitted. The in_domain_analysis block carries introduced_in_diff:true to confirm commit.md was changed in this diff. Out-of-domain files carry introduced_in_diff:true as provenance metadata.
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.default_safe_behavior: Per agent contract: absent/null/untagged file-level finding output is treated as pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking by close.md. Empty findings means close.md has nothing from prompt-inspector to block on for this diff.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) reviewed the draft empty-findings posture for the commands/commit.md Step 3 change. Verdict: empty findings list is defensible for the changed Step 3 hunk; codex explicitly stated 'I would not force a finding' on the added 5-line prose paragraph because it is procedural and load-bearing (conveys resolution order, helper location, exit-code contract, error-state variable retention). Codex offered one optional non-required compression of the added block and flagged 4 pre-existing verbose passages elsewhere in commit.md (lines 80-86, 135, 149-153, 196) — all of which fall outside --changed-files cleanliness scope per Section 5.4 rule 3.

findings.issue: Step 3 added block (commit.md:47-54) is compressible but procedural and load-bearing; codex would not force a finding.
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly stated 'I would not force a finding' for the in-scope diff hunk. Confirms empty findings list is correct for cleanliness scope.

findings.issue: Optional compression of Step 3 added block to a single sentence (style preference, not cleanliness violation)
findings.proposed_fix: Resolve CLOSE_REPORT with scripts/resolve-close-report.sh: prefer subproject docs/dev/, then /root/docs/dev/. On no match, the helper prints the fallback path and exits 1 so check 1 can emit the standard error.
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly tagged this 'Optional compression only, not required'. The current 5-line prose preserves the same 4 load-bearing rules; rewriting is style preference, not a cleanliness violation. No blocker exists when codex itself declares the change non-required.

findings.issue: Pre-existing rationale/story text at commit.md:135 ('operationalizes the user binding directive', 'verbatim Chinese phrasing preserved')
findings.proposed_fix: Current-cycle only: all glob and content predicates MUST be anchored to ${TASK_ID}; do not load or match prior-cycle artifacts.
findings.rationale: Out-of-scope for --changed-files cleanliness mode. Line 135 is NOT in the current diff; it is pre-existing content. Per the inspector's --changed-files scope contract (Section 5.4 rule 3): 'cleanliness scope = only violations newly introduced in this diff; pre-existing historical dirt is entirely ignored'. Logged for future full-repo review only — does not block close-gate.

findings.issue: Pre-existing redundant proof-line passage at commit.md:149-153 ('real-world close-report line proving this case', 'ALL THREE')
findings.proposed_fix: Remove the proof-line passage; the accepted forms are already defined by the regex at lines 145-148.
findings.rationale: Out-of-scope for --changed-files cleanliness mode. Lines 149-153 are NOT in the current diff; they are pre-existing content. Same scope-contract reasoning as previous item.

findings.issue: Pre-existing hook-internals narrative at commit.md:80-86 (grant timestamp block)
findings.proposed_fix: Reduce to: timezone-aware ISO-8601 UTC strings only; no epoch values; no naive datetimes; use the grant-writer script; regex must match.
findings.rationale: Out-of-scope for --changed-files cleanliness mode. Lines 80-86 are NOT in the current diff; they are pre-existing content. Same scope-contract reasoning.

findings.issue: Pre-existing long paragraph at commit.md:196 ('Reversal-rationale guidance')
findings.proposed_fix: Do not enforce reversal-rationale in /commit; changelog-analyst Phase 6 owns commit-message reversal rationale.
findings.rationale: Out-of-scope for --changed-files cleanliness mode. Line 196 is NOT in the current diff; it is pre-existing content. Same scope-contract reasoning.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex's primary verdict on the in-scope diff (Step 3 added block in commands/commit.md) confirms the empty findings list. Codex's pre-existing-content suggestions (4 items, lines 80-86, 135, 149-153, 196) are correctly out-of-scope for --changed-files cleanliness mode per Section 5.4 rule 3 and were rejected with rationale. No findings were added or removed as a result of codex feedback because codex assessment is concordant with the draft: in-scope diff = clean; pre-existing dirt = ignored for cleanliness-CLOSE purposes.
